title: AmmoRaccoon per-retailer caliber-paths config
description: Schema for caliber_paths/<retailer>.json — the version-controlled per-retailer caliber->[category URL] map (expansion #4, Step 2), the replacement for each scraper's inline CALIBER_PATHS literal. scraper_lib.load_caliber_paths() hand-validates against these rules at load time (no jsonschema dependency, mirroring how the generator validates calibers.json) and fails loudly on a malformed entry. The URLs here are NOT derivable from calibers.json, so this is a separate source of truth, not a *_gen.* artifact. No URL reaches status=active without passing the Step-3 validation harness.

retailer.description: Loader key = scraper short-name = file stem (e.g. 'targetsports'). load_caliber_paths('targetsports') reads this file.

retailer_slug.description: The retailers.slug DB value (e.g. 'target-sports'), stored alongside for cross-reference. Not load-bearing.

platform.description: Discovery platform family that owns this retailer (shopify | bigcommerce | woocommerce | generic-sitemap | classic-firearms-oneoff). Persisted here because the 2026-06-11 scoping audit's platform table was never written down.

base.description: Origin the relative paths hang off (e.g. 'https://www.targetsportsusa.com'). The scraper's own SITE_BASE must equal this.

comment.description: Plain-English provenance / format note (JSON has no comments).

discovery.description: How the URLs were (or will be) discovered — input for the Step-3 discovery adapters + re-validation drift watch.

sitemap.description: Sitemap URL/name when method=sitemap (e.g. 'product-categories-sitemap.xml (declared in robots.txt)').

category_url_pattern.description: Regex signature that distinguishes a caliber category URL in the sitemap (e.g. '-c-\d+\.aspx').

calibers.description: registry caliber slug -> LIST of category-URL entries. Always a list: one combined caliber can split into several upstream pages (.223 Rem + 5.56 NATO).

path.description: Category page path, no query string, leading slash. Joined to `base` (or the scraper's SITE_BASE) at fetch time.

query.description: Sort/paging suffix without the leading '?' (e.g. 'pp=240&SortOrder=PriceAscending'). Stored apart from path so the redirect guard can ignore it.

status.description: active = fetched by the scraper. candidate = discovered + validated, awaiting human sign-off (NEVER fetched). parked = known-bad / walled (NEVER fetched). load_caliber_paths returns only status=active by default.

expect_landed.description: Canonical landed path the request must resolve to — the redirect-guard / Step-3-validation target. Defaults to `path` when absent. Not used to alter runtime behavior (the runtime guard compares request-vs-landed).

title_filter.description: Optional case-insensitive regex applied to product titles on a mixed-caliber category page (e.g. gritr's 'other handgun calibers'). null when the page is single-caliber. Compiled by the loader; an invalid regex fails loudly.

validation.description: Result/provenance of the last validation. Booleans/status reflect known-true facts; measured numerics are null until the Step-3 harness records them (honest-by-default — never invented).

redirect.description: Whether the request redirected away from `path`.

title_match.description: Whether the page <title> names the caliber.

gate_pass_pct.description: Fraction of product titles that pass the caliber gate. null = not yet measured.

n_products.description: Product count on the page. null = not yet measured.
